metrics.metric_definition: Current market price of GME common stock, represented by the most recent closing price or intraday last-trade price.

source_bindings.freshness_cadence: end-of-day; intraday available during market hours
source_bindings.notes: Verified 2026-05-27: returned GME (GameStop Corp.) close=22.15 USD on NYSE. Free tier, no API key required for delayed data.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: GameStop Corp (GME) confirmed on Barchart overview page
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: Current price $22.14, plus open/high/low/close, volume, 52-week range
independent_comparator.evidence: Page shows GME spot price $22.14 with full OHLCV data. Price matches Yahoo Finance close within market delay tolerance. Independent source confirms same metric and methodology.

metrics.metric_definition: Open interest for each listed GME option contract, broken down by strike price and expiration date, separated into calls and puts.

source_bindings.notes: Verified 2026-05-27: yfinance returned 38 call strikes and 36 put strikes for nearest expiration (2026-05-29). 17 expirations available. OI confirmed non-zero across multiple strikes.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: GME Options Prices for Gamestop Corp Stock on Barchart
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: Open interest by strike for calls and puts, with bid/ask, volume, delta, IV per contract across multiple expirations
independent_comparator.evidence: Page displays full options chain with OI by strike for both calls and puts. Includes Put OI Total and Call OI Total aggregations. Independent source confirms same metric (OI per strike per expiration per option type).

metrics.metric_definition: Implied volatility for each listed GME option contract as quoted by the exchange, representing the market's expectation of future volatility embedded in option prices.

source_bindings.notes: Verified 2026-05-27: impliedVolatility field present and populated on every option row (e.g., ATM call IV ~2.55 for 2026-05-29 expiry). Values are annualized decimals.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: GME Options Volatility & Greeks for Gamestop Corp Stock on Barchart
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: Implied Volatility per option contract displayed alongside Greeks (delta, gamma, theta, vega, rho) per strike per expiration
independent_comparator.evidence: Page shows IV per strike per option type with full Greeks. IV defined as 'estimated volatility of the underlying stock over the period of the option'. Independent source confirms same metric and methodology.

metrics.metric_definition: 30-day implied volatility for GME, calculated as the interpolated at-the-money IV from the two nearest expiration dates that bracket 30 calendar days to expiry.

source_bindings.notes: Derived: select ATM options (strike nearest to spot) from the two expirations bracketing 30 DTE, then linearly interpolate their IV. Source fields (impliedVolatility, strike) verified available. Link status downgraded to proxy: Barchart shows an aggregate 'Implied Volatility' figure (48.55%) but does not disclose whether it uses the same 30-DTE ATM interpolation methodology.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: GME Options Volatility & Greeks for Gamestop Corp Stock on Barchart
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: Implied Volatility 48.55% shown as aggregate; methodology undisclosed (may not match our 30-DTE ATM linear interpolation)
independent_comparator.evidence: Barchart shows IV=48.55% for GME. Our IV30 is derived via ATM interpolation across expirations bracketing 30 DTE. Barchart's aggregate IV methodology is undisclosed. Directional validation only.

exhaustion_log: CBOE does not provide a free GME-specific IV30 index; IVX data requires paid subscription.
No free third-party API found providing pre-computed IV30 for individual equities.

metrics.metric_definition: 20-day historical (realized) volatility of GME, computed as the annualized standard deviation of the natural log of daily close-to-close returns over the trailing 20 trading days.

source_bindings.notes: Derived: HV20 = STDDEV(LN(close[t]/close[t-1])) over 20 trading days, annualized by * SQRT(252). Verified 62+ trading days of close prices available. Link status downgraded to proxy: Barchart shows 30-day HV (51.93%), not 20-day. Different lookback window means directional validation only.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: GME Options Volatility & Greeks for Gamestop Corp Stock on Barchart
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: Historical Volatility 51.93% shown as '30-day historic volatility for the underlying asset'
independent_comparator.evidence: Barchart shows 30-day HV=51.93% for GME. Our HV20 uses a 20-trading-day window. Different lookback windows (30-day vs 20-day) mean values will differ systematically. Directional validation only.

metrics.metric_definition: The strike price at which the total dollar value of outstanding GME options (calls + puts) would expire with the least value, representing the theoretical price that causes maximum loss for option holders.

source_bindings.notes: Derived: for each candidate strike K, sum intrinsic-value losses across all call OI (max(0, K-strike)*OI) and put OI (max(0, strike-K)*OI). The K that minimizes total losses is max pain. OI data verified available per strike. Link status downgraded to proxy: maximum-pain.com has a GME calculator but content is JavaScript-rendered; exact methodology not verifiable via static fetch.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: Stock Option Max Pain Calculator page; GME-specific data requires JavaScript rendering
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: Max pain calculator available but specific GME strike value not renderable via static HTTP fetch (JS-only SPA)
independent_comparator.evidence: Page returned HTTP 200 with max pain calculator framework. GME ticker is recognized by the site. However, actual max pain strike data is rendered client-side via JavaScript and was not present in static HTML. Cannot verify exact methodology match. Directional validation only if accessed interactively.

exhaustion_log: No free API provides pre-computed max pain for GME.

metrics.metric_definition: Put-to-call open interest ratio for GME options, computed as the total put open interest divided by total call open interest across all strikes for a given expiration (or aggregated across all expirations).

source_bindings.notes: Derived: SUM(put.openInterest) / SUM(call.openInterest). Both call and put OI fields verified present and populated across all strikes.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: GME Put/Call Ratios for Gamestop Corp Stock on Barchart
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: Put/Call Open Interest Ratio 0.35 (Put OI Total: 367,852; Call OI Total: 1,047,392). Also shows Put/Call Volume Ratio 0.11.
independent_comparator.evidence: Barchart shows Put/Call Open Interest Ratio = 0.35 for GME, computed as total put OI / total call OI. Same methodology as our derivation: SUM(put OI) / SUM(call OI). Independent source confirms exact metric match.

metrics.metric_definition: Net Gamma Exposure for GME, representing the aggregate gamma-weighted open interest across all strikes, indicating how much delta-hedging market makers must perform for a $1 move in the underlying.
metrics.expected_grain: daily per-symbol (and per-strike for GEX profile)

source_bindings.notes: Derived: gamma is computed via Black-Scholes (N'(d1) / (S * sigma * sqrt(T))) using per-strike IV, spot price, and DTE. GEX per strike = gamma * OI * 100 * spot. Net GEX = SUM(call_GEX) - SUM(put_GEX). All input fields verified available. Risk-free rate assumed from US Treasury yield [THEORETICAL]. Link status downgraded to proxy: Barchart GEX page exists for GME but chart data failed to load at verification time; methodology not verifiable.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: Gamestop Corp (GME) Gamma Exposure page on Barchart; page framework loaded with IV=48.55%, HV=51.93%, IV Rank=16.75%
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: Gamma Exposure chart framework present (call gamma vs put gamma by strike, net GEX) but data load failed with 'Error occurred while loading data'
independent_comparator.evidence: Barchart has a dedicated GEX page for GME with configurable expiration and strike parameters. Page confirmed GME identity (ticker, IV, HV metrics displayed). However, the actual GEX chart data returned a load error at capture time. Cannot verify values or exact methodology. Directional validation only when data loads successfully.

exhaustion_log: Yahoo Finance does not provide Greeks directly; must derive gamma from Black-Scholes.
CBOE provides Greeks on its website but not via a free structured API for GME specifically.

metrics.metric_definition: Percentile rank of the current IV30 relative to the trailing 252-trading-day (1-year) range of IV30, indicating whether current implied volatility is historically high or low for GME.

source_bindings.notes: Derived: IV Rank = (current_IV30 - min_IV30_252d) / (max_IV30_252d - min_IV30_252d). Requires the pipeline to accumulate daily IV30 snapshots over 252 trading days before this metric becomes fully accurate. Link status remains 'proxy': Barchart shows IV Rank=16.75% using their own IV baseline and 1-year lookback; our pipeline uses self-computed IV30 which may diverge. Additionally, the initial accumulation period uses a shorter lookback window.

independent_comparator.rendered_asset_identity: GME Options Volatility & Greeks for Gamestop Corp Stock on Barchart
independent_comparator.rendered_metric_identity: IV Rank 16.75% defined as 'The current IV compared to the highest and lowest values over the past 1-year'
independent_comparator.evidence: Barchart shows IV Rank=16.75% for GME using a 1-year lookback. Our IV Rank uses the same formula but is based on self-computed IV30 (which may differ from Barchart's IV baseline). During the initial accumulation period (<252 days), our lookback is shorter. Directional validation only.

exhaustion_log: No free provider offers pre-computed historical IV30 time series for individual equities.
CBOE IVX requires paid data subscription.
Self-accumulation from daily IV30 snapshots is the only viable free approach.
